title: README hero image must avoid long terminal labels
summary: README hero image is rendered small on GitHub, so long terminal strings and inspector titles overflow or become unreadable. Keep the launch image closer to the earlier kage demo.gif composition: short step labels, compac
body: README hero image is rendered small on GitHub, so long terminal strings and inspector titles overflow or become unreadable. Keep the launch image closer to the earlier kage-demo.gif composition: short step labels, compact metric tiles, concise terminal output, and visible website/viewer links in README.

context.fact: README hero images render small on GitHub, so long terminal labels and inspector titles become unreadable.
context.why: Launch media must remain legible at GitHub README scale, not only at full-size local preview.
context.trigger: Recall when editing README hero screenshots, SVG posters, or launch demo imagery.
context.action: Use short step labels, compact metric tiles, concise terminal output, and visible website/viewer links.
context.verification: Repo-local capture grounded to README.md and docs/assets/kage-viewer.svg.
context.risk_if_forgotten: Agents may create attractive full-size art that fails when rendered inside the README.
context.stale_when: GitHub README rendering changes or the hero is replaced by a media format that does not shrink labels.
